message:
feat(fcp-core): pin canonical operation tracing span contract

Promote the operation-level tracing span from ad-hoc per-call
construction to a single canonical helper plus a normative attribute
list. This is the contract every host/middleware/connector that emits
operation-level telemetry must follow, and the contract every
log/trace consumer (Grafana boards, Tempo dashboards, jaeger query
filters) is now allowed to assume.

Production code (crates/fcp-core/src/operation.rs):

- `CANONICAL_OPERATION_SPAN_NAME = "fcp_operation"` — the only span
  name callers should use for canonical operation execution. Hardcoded
  string literal so dashboards can match on it without touching the
  build system.
- `CANONICAL_OPERATION_SPAN_ATTRIBUTE_NAMES` — `[zone_id,
  capability_id, principal_id, instance_id, request_id]`, the five
  fields a canonical span MUST carry. The order in the array also
  reflects the canonical emission order so consumers can rely on it
  for column ordering in flat-file exports.
- `canonical_operation_span(zone_id, capability_id, principal_id,
  instance_id, request_id)` — `#[must_use]` constructor that emits the
  span with the five attributes formatted via `Display`. Existing
  callers can drop in this helper instead of hand-rolling
  `tracing::info_span!` invocations and risking field-name drift.
- Import block grows to bring `CapabilityId`, `InstanceId`,
  `PrincipalId`, and `RequestId` into scope.
- A handful of `assert!` reformat artifacts in the test module for the
  receipt-intent binding tests are folded in here rather than carried
  separately; they are pure rustfmt churn alongside the new code.

Conformance test (crates/fcp-core/tests/tracing_span_contract.rs):

- Stand-up a minimal `Subscriber` + `Visit` capture harness so the
  test does not depend on the global registry.
- Assert that `canonical_operation_span(...)` emits a span whose
  metadata `name()` equals `CANONICAL_OPERATION_SPAN_NAME`.
- Assert the visited field set equals the documented stable contract
  `[zone_id, capability_id, principal_id, instance_id, request_id]`
  in the documented order — this is what blocks future refactors from
  silently renaming or reordering fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
